Inside Out 2 mistakes emotional awareness for emotional surrender. While the animation is polished and the voice performances are solid, the film’s central message is deeply confused. By elevating Anxiety to a dominant guiding force, the movie normalizes fear, overthinking, and emotional paralysis without ever meaningfully challenging them. Instead of teaching kids how to manage anxiety, the film repeatedly validates it as reasonable, inevitable, and self-directing. Fear isn’t something to confront or regulate; it’s something to listen to. The result is a story that frames emotional instability as identity rather than a state to grow through. For a studio once known for pairing emotional honesty with resilience, this is a troubling shift. Like many recent releases from The Walt Disney Company, the film prioritizes emotional affirmation over emotional strength. Inside Out 2 isn’t empowering, it’s indulgent. It teaches kids what they feel, but not how to rise above it.

More of the same Inside Out story nonsense as we follow the protagonist into puberty. The animation and rendering are technically brilliant, and nobody can argue with a film that made nearly 1.5 billion (with a “b”) at the box office. I'm sure a million parents thought this was a good way to bond with their kids, but to me, it’s just more overlapping dramedic monotony between the differing bits of personality for 96 minutes in hopes of selling merch.

Ayant vécu des crises d’angoisse pendant des années, je trouve que Pixar a magnifiquement bien mis en image à quoi peut ressembler une de ces crises de l’intérieur. Mes proches ont pu réaliser ce que je pouvais vivre et rien que pour ça, merci. Au-delà de ça, c’est intéressant de voir l’évolution de Riley dans cette nouvelle étape de sa vie. Ce deuxième film ne vaut cependant pas le premier. Petite mention pour le doublage de Dorothé Pousséo en tant qu’anxiété qui est parfait !

I've bee a fan of the inside out franchise since the first movie came out, and I was using this film to help me navigate through hard times, as it showed me so many interesting concepts of the mind. This inside out sequel takes all of this elements and gives them a spin. With the addition of new emotions and the sense of self, this movie teaches us once again to accept every bit of ourself in a new-refreshing way. Even though the pacing was -in my opinion- sometimes a bit fast, it helps develop the story so it can hit pixar's hour and thirty minutes mark for most of their films. so fun!!

All Time Classic. Really well made overall. If were comparing it to the first I ould still give it to the first which as more emotional impact and a more cohesive story. This can feel like 2 seperate films the majority of the time. And the journey of our main 5 is retrending the same locations we've seen previously. All the new emotions are good but it feels like outside of anxiety they don't get the same level of development. Some of them are more memorable than others. But it's loaded with tons of character development, a great message, lots of underlying commentary, and truly does what a sequel is suppposed to do in first rate form and it earns a point for that. Everyone should give this a try once.
